vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter (or column slot on bench-seat trims)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Cap at side of console shifter (or column slot on bench-seat trims).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: Same platform as Silverado. 4WD: transfer case to 2H.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (Autotrac) or manual floor lever depending on year and trim
positions: 2WD (2H) — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi (4H) — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo (4L) — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: ELECTRIC: Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2WD button. Confirm 2WD indicator.
MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC lever to 2H.
Confirm 2WD indicator before lifting rear wheels.
NEVER tow in 4H, 4WD Auto, or 4L on dry pavement.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Transfer Case in 4H/4L Will Destroy Driveline
knownIssues.detail: Same platform as Silverado 1500. Towing in 4H or 4L on dry pavement causes severe transfer case and differential damage. Confirm 2WD indicator is ON before lifting. The most common towing damage claim on this platform.

battery.warnings: Confirm TC in 2WD before wheel lift — most common towing damage on this platform.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.warn: Sierra EV: FLATBED ONLY. 4WD: transfer case.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial on center console (Autotrac)
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn the rotary TC dial to 2WD.
Confirm the 2WD indicator on the instrument cluster.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap at console shifter or electronic selector slot depending on year
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap at console shifter or electronic selector slot depending on year.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Duramax diesel may have dual batteries.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Duramax diesel: same. Heavy-duty — heavy blocks.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Heavy-duty — use rated equipment. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button or manual lever (varies by year)
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: ELECTRIC: Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H. Confirm indicator.
MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC lever to 2H.
Confirm before lifting rear wheels.
Heavy-duty truck — use proper rated equipment.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.location: Under hood. Duramax diesel may have dual batteries.
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red (+) on either battery, black (−) to engine block
battery.warnings: Diesel dual batteries. Very heavy — verify equipment ratings.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap at side of console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap at side of console shifter base.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case. Large — block all wheels.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the dash or console.
Confirm 2WD indicator lamp illuminates.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap near shifter base (2010-2017) or electronic selector slot (2018+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near shifter base (2010-2017) or electronic selector slot (2018+).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.

neutral: Apply parking brake.

tc.type: Electric push-button or rotary dial on console
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Select 2H on transfer case control.
Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
4WD: TC to 2H before wheel-lift.
vehicles.notes: S-15 Jimmy. Same platform as Chevy Blazer S10.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: TC to 2H, then lift from rear.

positions: 2H
4H
4L
steps: Stop completely.
Move TC to 2H.
Confirm before lifting.

towNotes: RWD: lift from rear. 4WD: TC to 2H.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on floor console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on floor console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: AWD uses Borg-Warner AWD system — no traditional TC. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear. AWD: flatbed preferred.

vehicles.trans: 4-speed automatic (4L60-E) or 5-speed automatic (5L40-E for I6 inline-6)
vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.notes: Shares Trailblazer platform. Electronic TC push-button. Must be in 2WD for road towing.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2WD before road towing. 4WD on dry pavement damages driveline.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2WD first, then lift rear.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
AWD — automatic — NOT safe for road towing
4WD Hi — part-time — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — NEVER tow on road
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the dashboard.
Confirm 2WD indicator is lit.
Verify all 4WD indicator lights are off before lifting rear wheels.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, shift TC to 2WD, then disconnect.

towNotes: Electronic TC requires 12V power to shift.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect. If unable, FLATBED ONLY.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on floor console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on floor console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD/2H before road towing.
vehicles.notes: S-10 Blazer/S-Series platform. Manual TC floor lever on most. Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.warn: Shift TC to 2H before road towing.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2H first, then lift rear.

positions: 2WD — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Hi — NOT safe for dry road
4WD Lo — NEVER tow on road
steps: Stop completely.
Move TC floor lever to 2WD.
Confirm 2WD before lifting rear wheels.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch, select Neutral.

towNotes: Manual TC — shift to 2WD before road towing.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch, select Neutral.
